1.name: Spellcasting
1.description: Cast bard spells using Charisma. Know a fixed number of spells and cantrips based on level.

1.name: Bardic Inspiration
1.description: Bonus action: give one creature within 60 ft a Bardic Inspiration die (d6, scales to d12). Spend it to add to one ability check, attack, or saving throw within 10 minutes. Uses = CHA mod per long rest (short rest at level 5).

2.name: Jack of All Trades
2.description: Add half proficiency bonus (rounded down) to any ability check you're not already adding your proficiency bonus to.

2.name: Song of Rest
2.description: During a short rest, friendly creatures who hear your performance regain extra HP when spending hit dice (1d6, scales to 1d12 at level 17).

3.name: Bard College
3.description: Choose a subclass: Lore, Valor, Glamour, Swords, Whispers, Creation, Eloquence, or others.

3.name: Expertise
3.description: Double proficiency bonus for two chosen skill proficiencies.

5.name: Font of Inspiration
5.description: Regain all Bardic Inspiration uses on a short or long rest.

6.name: Countercharm
6.description: Action: start a performance. Until end of your next turn, you and friendly creatures within 30 ft have advantage on saves vs being frightened or charmed.

10.name: Magical Secrets
10.description: Learn two spells from any class. They count as bard spells for you. Learn two more at levels 14 and 18.

20.name: Superior Inspiration
20.description: If you have no Bardic Inspiration uses left when you roll initiative, regain one use.
